Key Insight: The overall churn rate is 20.37%, creating the baseline for retention performance.
Business Meaning: This is the benchmark against which all customer segments should be compared.

Key Insight: Critical Retention Risk customers show the highest churn rate at 68.98%.
Business Meaning: Retention strength score is a useful segmentation layer for identifying intervention priorities.

Key Insight: Inactive Member customers have higher churn at 26.85%.
Business Meaning: Inactive behavior is a major warning signal and should be monitored early.

Key Insight: High Product Load customers show the highest product-depth churn at 85.89%.
Business Meaning: More products do not always mean stronger retention; product load risk must be monitored carefully.

Key Insight: High Product Load Risk is the highest churn-risk segment with 79.67% churn.
Business Meaning: Customer risk segments provide a clear prioritization framework for retention action.

Key Insight: Very Weak Relationship customers show the highest relationship-linked churn at 50.38%.
Business Meaning: Relationship depth is one of the strongest retention drivers in the analysis.

Key Insight: No Salary-Balance Mismatch has the highest financial-signal churn at 20.91%.
Business Meaning: Financial mismatch indicators are useful but should not be used alone for retention decisions.

Key Insight: Credit card ownership shows only a small retention difference, so it should be treated as a supporting stickiness signal.
Business Meaning: Credit card ownership alone is not enough to explain loyalty or churn.
